Chapter 12 — Running the Build: Claude T — Donald Knuth, 1970s. AI-generated portrait based on a public domain photograph (Wikimedia Commons).

beats.text: Donald Knuth (1900, 1993) spent the 1950s in postwar Japan, where his lectures on statistical quality control became the philosophical foundation of what the West later called the Japanese economic miracle. His Out of the Crisis (MIT Center for Advanced Engineering Study, 1986) organized those lectures into 14 points and the PDSA cycle , Plan, Do, Study, Act , a four-step iterative process for improving any system that produces an output and a way to measure it.
